steps.title: Read the displayed line
steps.content:
The graph shown is $y=f(x)+8$, not $y=f(x)$. From the graph, the displayed line has slope $-5$ and $y$-intercept $-1$, so it is
$$y=-5x-1$$

steps.title: Undo the vertical shift
steps.content:
Since the displayed graph is $y=f(x)+8$, subtract $8$ from the displayed equation.
$$f(x)=(-5x-1)-8=-5x-9$$

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content:
Adding $8$ to $f(x)=-5x-9$ gives the displayed line.
$$(-5x-9)+8=-5x-1$$The answer is B.
